title: hieracium viscosum subsp. scariolifolium
intro: Hieracium viscosum subsp. scariolifolium, also known as sticky hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Hieracium viscosum subsp. scariolifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
